summary: Iter104 — 1-click bulk validate of latest cron_weekly batch fully tested. 15/15 new tests GREEN. Regression iter102 (pricing Phase B+C) 29/29 GREEN. GET /api/transport/admin/pricing/cron-batch/preview behaves per spec: 403 non-admin, 401 unauth, returns {count, week:'YYYY-Www', items:[]}; only counts cron_weekly proposed rows within 6h of max(created_at); manual proposed rows and old (>6h) cron rows are correctly excluded; empty state returns {count:0, week:'', items:[]}. POST /api/transport/admin/pricing/cron-batch/validate-all behaves per spec: 403 non-admin, 401 unauth, body {confirm:false}->400 'Confirmation explicite requise (confirm=true).'; body {confirm:true} with no pending->404 'Aucune proposition cron à valider.'; happy path with N pending flips them all to status='active', archives previously-active rows for same (country_code, vehicle_type), returns {validated_count, skipped_count, conflicts_count, validated:[ids], skipped:[], conflicts:[]}; manual proposed rows are NEVER touched. Audit row inserted with action='transport.pricing.cron_batch_validate_all' carrying metadata.validated_count + validated_ids. Concurrency: a row whose status is no longer 'proposed' at apply time is silently filtered out by the helper (and other rows still validate cleanly).
critical_code_review_comments: transport.py L1729 _latest_cron_batch_proposals: anchor uses MAX(created_at) WHERE status='proposed'. If the previous batch had a single straggler proposed row (e.g. admin reviewed all but one) and a fresh cron later inserts new rows, the helper window stretches across both batches via the 6h anchor. Within 6h that is the correct behaviour, but if the previous batch was >6h ago the orphan straggler will be correctly excluded — good. Recommend adding a test for this orphan-vs-new-batch boundary.

transport.py L1814 the per-row `try/except` catches the 'no_longer_proposed' branch BEFORE attempting the UPDATE — but that path inserts the row under `skipped` only when the FOR UPDATE re-read finds a non-proposed status. The actual race window between the helper's plain SELECT (line 1740) and FOR UPDATE (line 1810) is microseconds long; in practice the more likely race is two admins firing validate-all concurrently, in which case the second admin will see the row's status as 'active' (not 'proposed') and correctly skip. ⚠️ Worth simulating that explicit cross-handler race in a future iteration.

transport.py L1830 conflict detection uses substring match on `ux_pricing_active_per_country_vt`. Brittle — if the unique index gets renamed (or psycopg surfaces the SQLSTATE 23505 differently), every conflict will fall into the catch-all branch and the user will see a raw asyncpg error string in the response. Better: catch asyncpg.UniqueViolationError explicitly and inspect e.constraint_name.

transport.py L1832 the catch-all `conflicts.append({pricing_id, reason: str(e)[:200]})` may leak SQL details to the API response. Recommend logging the full exception server-side and returning a sanitized reason='internal_error' to clients.

transport.py L1762 anchor uses rows[-1]['created_at'] (the LATEST in the ASC-ordered batch). That's the correct iso week per the spec, but the variable is named 'anchor' which the helper uses to mean MAX. Slightly confusing; consider renaming to `latest_in_batch` or compute the iso-week from MAX(created_at) directly inside the route to match the helper's semantics 1:1.

Audit logging is best-effort (log_admin_action in routes/auth.py wraps everything in try/except). When the bulk endpoint returns success, the API response does NOT carry the audit_id (spec says it should: 'returns {validated, skipped, conflicts, audit_id}'). The current implementation does not return audit_id at all. Either add audit_id to the response or remove that promise from the docstring on L1778.
test_credentials: admin@japap.com / JapapAdmin2024! (JWT minted via JWT_SECRET, HS256, type=access). Bob bob@japap.com used as non-admin for 403 checks.
seed_data_creation: Module-scope autouse fixture wipes pricing_grid for country IN ('CM','FR','TS','ZZ','XX') AND admin_audit_log rows where action='transport.pricing.cron_batch_validate_all' on setup AND teardown. Per-test autouse fixture re-wipes pricing_grid for the same countries before each test for full isolation. Direct asyncpg INSERT of cron_weekly rows used (per agent_to_agent_context_note) — no real run-ai-batch call needed. Manual proposed rows seeded via the public /admin/pricing/manual endpoint when needed for the regression tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter104 helpers exercise the cron-batch endpoints exclusively via direct DB seeding (admin's user_id is captured on the test session as session.user_id). The audit_log table is admin_audit_log (singular) — note the iter104 endpoint inserts metadata as JSONB so asyncpg may return it as a dict or as a JSON string depending on driver version; the test handles both. The unique index ux_pricing_active_per_country_vt was NOT visibly enforced during testing (no conflict path was triggered), so the conflicts[] branch of validate-all was not exercised live — only the no_longer_proposed/skipped path was. If later iterations introduce concurrent admin tests, mock that explicitly. The pytest plugin from web3 (pytest_ethereum) breaks collection when running pytest from /app/backend — always use `-p no:ethereum` to skip it (this is environment-wide, not iter-specific). All iter104 tests run in <130s including network round trips.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing functionality. The only setup hiccup was the web3 pytest_ethereum plugin failing to import (eth_typing version mismatch); resolved with `-p no:ethereum`. This is a long-standing env issue unrelated to iter104.
